emails.subject: Re: natsource fees
emails.body: Re: natsource fees I've checked with Matt Motley and changed 458712 and 458718 to reflect broker fees of .0075. I've left a message for Mike Swerzbin to double-check. He has 457489 entered in his log book with no broker fee, but 459812 says nothing either way; so I'd like to get ahold of him before I change anything. Thanks, Kate Kerri Thompson@ENRON 11/22/2000 09:19 AM

emails.subject: Re: natsource fees
emails.body: Both 457489 and 459812 were logged in Mike's trade book with "no broker fee" written in the margin. I checked with Mike Driscoll, who helped Swerzbin enter the deals, who verified that these are sleeves of other deals and do not require broker fees. Let me know if Natsource still disputes this. Thanks, Kate Kerri Thompson@ENRON 11/28/2000 12:52 PM

emails.subject: Re: natsource fees
emails.body: Re: natsource fees These deals are all parts of sleeves we did for EES, and we only include a broker fee on the sale side of the sleeve. I ended up changing just two deals; they're highlighted in red: 459812 no change (buy side of EES sleeve) 459813 added .0075 broker fee (sale side of EES sleeve) 457489 no change (buy side of EES sleeve) 457490 no change (sale side already contained .0075 broker fee) 468444 added .0075 broker fee (sale side) 468440 no change (buy side) And as a disclaimer I'm getting my information from our traders, so if any of this sounds incorrect, or if Natsource still disputes, please let me know. Thanks, Kate Kerri Thompson@ENRON 01/10/2001 12:07 PM
